target.name: Theseus
target.founder: Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, Sacha Lévy

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Theseus is a Seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers (2 public, 8 private), including same-sector and adjacent-stage companies such as Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Tenna Systems.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the four-company peer set is $225M, with a range spanning $60M (Tenna Systems, Seed) to $500M (Stark Defence, Series A). This method reflects the typical valuation density for Defense & Security companies at Seed and Series A stages. The comparable set includes both earlier-stage benchmarks (Tenna Systems at $60M) and more mature peers (Stark Defence at $500M), establishing a reasonable bracket for a Seed-stage entrant.

The triangulated valuation range is $191M (low), $225M (median), and $259M (high). This range is supported by the peer-median method alone; wider triangulation is constrained by limited data across alternative methodologies. Key uncertainties include product-market fit validation, differentiation relative to the broader Anduril/Scale AI/Saronic competitive set, and the extent to which Seed-stage capital efficiency in this sector diverges from the Series A comps in the peer set. The median estimate of $225M assumes Theseus commands valuation parity with the peer cohort's central tendency.
